channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ekurhuleni Deputy Chief Denies Refusal to Submit Warning Statement in Precious Stones Case
item.description: Deputy Chief of the Ekurhuleni Metro Police Department (EMPD), Julius Mkhwanazi, appeared before the Madlanga Commission on Wednesday, refuting claims that he had refused to submit a warning statement...

item.title: Snapchat's Parent Company Reduces Workforce Due to AI Adoption
item.description: Snapchat’s parent company, a leading social media giant, recently announced cuts to its workforce, impacting 1,000 employees. The company attributes these reductions to increased efficiency resulting ...

item.title: How a network of individuals and institutions enable money laundering in Kenya
item.description: The article discusses how money laundering in Kenya is sustained by a web of powerful actors who benefit from keeping the system weak and opaque. It argues that this is not just about individual crimi...

item.title: Bernie Sanders Calls for US to End Military Support for Israel Over Gaza Conflict
item.description: US Senator Bernie Sanders, a Jewish American with a personal family history tied to the Holocaust, has publicly called for an end to US military aid to Israel, citing the actions of Prime Minister Ben...

item.title: Construction of Monterazzas de Cebu to restart after delays
item.description: The developer of Monterazzas de Cebu has announced that construction work will soon resume on the large-scale residential project in Cebu City. The project, which faced long delays following the devas...

item.title: India and US Strengthen Bilateral Energy Cooperation Amid Global Market Uncertainty
item.description: Union Minister for Petroleum and Natural Gas, Hardeep Singh Puri, held a productive meeting with US Ambassador Sergio Gor in New Delhi to enhance the strategic energy partnership between India and the...

item.title: David Lammy and Starmer’s team share responsibility for shaping UK foreign policy
item.description: David Lammy, currently serving as the UK’s Justice Secretary and Deputy Prime Minister, continues to play a significant role in foreign affairs despite no longer being Foreign Secretary. His energetic...

item.title: Draft plan released for North Curtin residential and diplomatic precinct
item.description: The National Capital Authority (NCA) has released a draft plan for the North Curtin Residential Area, signalling a major transformation of the Curtin horse paddocks. Covering 13 hectares, the plan pro...

item.title: Psychiatrists debate potential uptake of MAID for mental illness in Canada
item.description: Canada is currently preparing to expand Medical Assistance in Dying (MAID) to include individuals whose sole underlying condition is a mental illness, scheduled for March 2027. During a special joint ...

item.title: Cassandre Prosper becomes first Quebec-born player drafted into the WNBA
item.description: Cassandre Prosper, a native of Montreal, has made history by becoming the first Quebec-born basketball player to be drafted into the WNBA. Prosper was selected in the second round of the WNBA draft by...

item.title: Israel kills three Lebanese paramedics during ongoing truce negotiations in Lebanon
item.description: In the middle of ceasefire efforts between Lebanon and Israel, Israeli forces carried out fresh strikes in southern Lebanon and killed at least three paramedics. Lebanese health authorities said Israe...

item.title: EPRA lowers fuel prices after brief spike, offers slight relief to Kenyan motorists
item.description: The Energy and Petroleum Regulatory Authority (EPRA) has reduced fuel prices in Kenya just one day after announcing a sharp increase, giving consumers a small reprieve at the pump. The latest review, ...

item.title: APC Chairman Assures Party Stability Despite Incoming Defections Ahead of 2027 Elections
item.description: The National Chairman of APC, Nentawe Yilwatda, don dey calm Nigerians say the recent defections from opposition parties no go cause wahala for the ruling party. During one media chat for Abuja, he ya...

item.title: Security personnel protecting polio teams attacked in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa and Balochistan
item.description: ISLAMABAD: During the ongoing nationwide polio immunisation drive, over 27 million children out of the targeted 45 million have received vaccinations so far. However, the Pakistan Polio Eradication In...

item.title: Manitoba prepares for busy camping season as bookings begin after wildfire disruptions
item.description: Manitoba has officially opened campsite bookings for the upcoming summer season, marking a much-anticipated return to outdoor recreation following last year’s destructive wildfires. Local retailers su...

item.title: Addressing the Growing Crisis of Bullying in South African Schools
item.description: Bullying in South African schools has escalated into a national crisis affecting nearly 40% of learners, according to the 1000 Women Trust. Recent incidents, including the death of an eight-year-old i...

item.title: Gibbons works to stabilise finances after $15.3M debt
item.description: The town of Gibbons, Alberta, with a population of around 3,300, is confronting significant financial challenges after inheriting a $15.3 million debt from the previous council. The new administration...

item.title: Australia beat Kenya 2‑0 to win inaugural FIFA Women’s Series in Nairobi
item.description: Kenya’s Harambee Starlets narrowly missed out on lifting the inaugural FIFA Women’s Series 2026 title after they were beaten 2‑0 by Australia in the final at Nyayo National Stadium in Nairobi. The Mat...

item.title: Arsenal advance to Champions League semi-finals after narrow win over Sporting
item.description: Arsenal don secure dem place for UEFA Champions League semi-finals after dem manage draw 0-0 with Sporting CP, finishing 1-0 on aggregate from first-leg goal wey Kai Havertz score for Portugal. For Em...

item.title: Waymo plans to test self-driving taxis in Toronto amid regulatory and weather challenges
item.description: Waymo, the autonomous taxi operator owned by Alphabet, is exploring expansion into the Canadian market, with Toronto as a potential testing ground. The company plans to apply to Ontario's automated ve...
